_meta.subtaskTitle: Study Tawhid al-Asma wa al-Sifat — Names and Attributes
_meta.parentTaskTitle: Learn about the categories of Tawhid (Rububiyyah, Uluhiyyah, Asma wa Sifat)
_meta.claudeScholarQuery: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Study Tawhid al-Asma wa al-Sifat — Names and Attributes' under the parent task 'Learn about the categories of Tawhid (Rububiyyah, Uluhiyyah, Asma wa Sifat)' (module: faith_shahada_growth), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus.

muslimScholarQueries.query: What are the primary ayat, particularly Surah Al-A'raf 7:180 which commands invoking Allah by His most beautiful names and Surah Ash-Shura 42:11 which establishes that there is nothing like Him, that directly anchor the scriptural foundation for Tawhid al-Asma wa al-Sifat?

Which authentic narrations in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, specifically the definitive hadith stating that Allah has ninety-nine names and whoever preserves them will enter Paradise, contextually anchor the theological study of His divine Names and Attributes?

sources.ref: Sahih Bukhari, Volume 3, Book 50, Number 894 (Narrated Abu Huraira)
sources.translation: Allah has ninety-nine names, i.e. one-hundred minus one, and whoever knows (preserves) them will go to Paradise.
sources.rationale: MS q2 cit. 1/2 returns the canonical Bukhari ref with the operative clause quoted verbatim in the MS answer body: 'Allah has ninety-nine names, i.e. one-hundred minus one'. This is the definitive Prophetic statement establishing the study of Allah's Names as a category with a fixed count and a salvific reward — the precise content of the subtask. Matn drawn verbatim from MS answer text; reference chunk supplied isnad header only.

notebookTrace.query: Which authentic narrations in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, specifically the definitive hadith stating that Allah has ninety-nine names and whoever preserves them will enter Paradise, contextually anchor the theological study of His divine Names and Attributes?

_groundedBarCheck.reason: 1 entry (Sahih Bukhari Vol 3 Book 50 Number 894, Bayyinah, direct — matn clause quoted verbatim by MS). Bar met via hadith axis. Quran axis rejected: q1 answer named 42:11, 7:180, 59:22-24, 112:1-4, 20:8 — but all reference chunks returned were generic fragments ('10. And in anything over which you disag-', 'livestock; rather, they are more astray...') with no verse-level matn bound to any of the named ayat. Also rejected from q2: source c1b0a057 '99 names of Allah' is a non-canonical list document (title-only 23-char chunk), not a Quran/Hadith primary source — excluded per schema §3.
